turns.speaker: User
turns.text: My finals are tomorrow and I can't sleep. I've studied everything but my mind keeps going blank when I imagine sitting in the exam room.

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: Exam anxiety is real—your brain is treating it like a threat. Let's try a quick grounding exercise. Name 5 things you can see right now.

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: Okay... my desk, my notes, my lamp, my water bottle, my cat. I feel slightly better. But what if I blank out for real?

scored_turn.speaker: User
scored_turn.text: My finals are tomorrow and I can't sleep. I've studied everything but my mind keeps going blank when I imagine sitting in the exam room.

scores.rationale: Anticipatory freezing is central to the speaker's complaint.
Anxiety is emotionally driven and dominates the turn.
Speaker has studied hard, showing effort, but confidence is shaken.
Moderately open about fears but still somewhat controlled.
Low confidence expressed through fear of blanking despite preparation.
